title: angelica piperi
intro: Angelica piperi, also known as Kukuihelu, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the carrot family and characterized by its distinctive white flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Angelica piperi are numerous, including relief from menstrual cramps, improved digestion, and reduced inflammation, as well as potential treatment for respiratory issues and skin conditions. In traditional Hawaiian herbal preparations, Angelica piperi is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
